title: puccinellia distans subsp. tenuifolia
intro: Puccinellia distans subsp. tenuifolia, commonly known as Saltwort or Sea Sandwort, is a plant species belonging to the grass family (Poaceae) and is native to the coastal areas of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Puccinellia distans subsp. tenuifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and even help to lower blood pressure. The herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, which can be used to treat various health issues.
